name: Jormungandr
description: Jormungandr, also known as the Midgard Serpent or World Serpent, is a colossal sea serpent from Norse mythology. He is one of the three monstrous children of Loki and the giantess Angrboða, along with the wolf Fenrir and the goddess Hel. Odin, fearing the power of these offspring, cast Jormungandr into the great ocean that encircles Midgard, the realm of humans. There, the serpent grew to such immense size that he encircled the entire world, grasping his own tail. This act gave him the name 'Midgard Serpent' as he literally holds the world within his coils. Jormungandr's existence is intrinsically linked to Ragnarok, the Norse apocalypse. During Ragnarok, Jormungandr will rise from the ocean, unleashing massive tidal waves and poisoning the land with his venom. He will engage in a deadly battle with Thor, the god of thunder, and though Thor will ultimately slay the serpent, he will succumb to the serpent's venom shortly thereafter. Jormungandr represents the untamed power of the ocean and the destructive forces of nature, a constant threat to the order of the cosmos. The serpent's immense size and venomous nature make him a formidable foe, embodying the chaotic and unpredictable aspects of the Norse world. His role in Ragnarok signifies the cyclical nature of destruction and rebirth, a fundamental concept in Norse cosmology. The serpent's connection to Loki further emphasizes his disruptive influence on the established order of the gods.
subtitle: The World Serpent, Coiled Around Midgard
appearance: Jormungandr is depicted as an enormous serpent, so large that he encircles the entire world of Midgard. His scales are typically described as dark and glistening, reflecting the depths of the ocean. Some accounts suggest he possesses bioluminescent properties, illuminating the dark waters with an eerie glow. His eyes are said to be cold and calculating, reflecting his ancient and malevolent nature. The serpent's mouth is filled with rows of sharp, venomous teeth, capable of inflicting fatal wounds. His sheer size is his most defining feature, making him a truly awe-inspiring and terrifying sight.
behavior: Jormungandr is a destructive and malevolent creature. He is driven by instinct and a primal desire to disrupt the order of the world. He is largely dormant, lying in the depths of the ocean, but when roused, he unleashes his full power upon the world. During Ragnarok, he becomes actively destructive, rising from the sea to participate in the final battle. He is a force of chaos and destruction, a symbol of the untamed power of nature.

keyMyths.title: The Fishing Trip
keyMyths.description: Thor attempts to catch Jormungandr with the giant Hymir, nearly succeeding before Hymir cuts the line in fear. This event highlights Thor's strength and Jormungandr's immense power.

keyMyths.title: Ragnarok
keyMyths.description: Jormungandr rises from the sea, poisons the land, and battles Thor during Ragnarok. Both are slain in the conflict, signifying the end of an era.

sources.author: Snorri Sturluson
sources.description: A key source for Norse mythology, providing detailed accounts of Jormungandr and his role in Ragnarok.
sources.text: A key source for Norse mythology, providing detailed accounts of Jormungandr and his role in Ragnarok.

sources.author: Various Poets
sources.description: A collection of Old Norse poems that offer insights into Norse mythology, including the tale of Thor's fishing trip.
